title: Variables
description: Meet x as a name for an unknown amount you already know how to find.

scaffolded.title: Meet x
scaffolded.body: You already know how to find a mystery weight on a balance scale. In algebra we just give that mystery a name: **x**. Tap through to see that a name changes nothing about the puzzle.

steps.text: Here a single mystery block sits alone and balances 7 pounds. Let's call the block x.
Because x sits all by itself, it must weigh exactly what's on the other pan.
So here x = 7. The letter is just a nickname for the amount you're hunting for. Your turn!

scaffolded.prompt: In algebra, **x** can stand for a mystery amount. If x balances 6 pounds, what is x?
scaffolded.hint: x is just a nickname for the mystery block. Since it sits all alone, whatever balances it IS its value.

feedback.correct: Exactly! x sits all alone, so it's worth whatever balances it, 6 pounds. You just read your first variable.
incorrect: Not quite. The x block is alone, so it equals the weight across from it.
Look at the right side of the balanced scale.

explanation.title: x is a name
explanation.body: A variable is just a name for an unknown amount, nothing more, so a lone x is worth exactly what sits across from it.

scaffolded.title: When x has extra weight
scaffolded.body: Often x isn't alone, it has a known weight sitting with it. To uncover x, take that same weight off **both** pans, exactly like before. Try the move.

interaction.resultCaption: The right pan drops from 11 to 8, the extra 3 leaves the left, and now x stands alone balancing 8, so here x = 8.

scaffolded.prompt: x plus 2 pounds balances 8 pounds. Change the scale to get x by itself, keep both pans balanced!

feedback.correct: Nice, you took the same 2 off both sides to keep it fair, and x came out to 6.
incorrect: Check what is left on the right after removing 2 from 8.
x is alone now. It equals the remaining weight.

scaffolded.hint: The +2 is just extra weight riding along with x. Take that same 2 off each side to set x free.

feedback.correct: Beautiful, both pans stayed equal the whole way, so x = 6.
incorrect: That changes only one side. Keep both sides equal.
Remove 2 from both trays, then read what x balances.

explanation.title: Lift off the extra weight
explanation.body: A number added to x is just along for the ride, so removing it equally from both sides frees x without tipping the scale.

scaffolded.prompt: If x + 1 = 5, what does x equal?
scaffolded.hint: x and the 1 are sitting together to balance 5. Just like the scale, take 1 off both sides so x is on its own, then see what is left.

feedback.correct: Yes! You undid the +1, so x = 4. Smart move.
incorrect: x plus 1 makes 5, so take that extra 1 back off to find x.

explanation.title: Get x by itself
explanation.body: An equation behaves like a balance: undo what is added to x on both sides and x ends up alone.

scaffolded.prompt: Two x blocks balance 10 pounds. What does one x block equal?
scaffolded.hint: Both blocks are the same x, so they each carry an equal share. Share the 10 evenly between the two of them.

feedback.correct: Exactly! Two equal x blocks split 10, so each x is 5. Nice splitting.
incorrect: Split 10 equally between the two matching x blocks.

explanation.title: Same variable, same value
explanation.body: Every copy of the same variable weighs the same, so together they split the total into equal shares.

mastery.prompt: If x balances 9 pounds, x equals...

feedback.correct: Yes! x is on its own, so it's just what it balances, 9.
incorrect: x is alone, so read the weight across from it.

feedback.correct: Right, clear the 3 first, and x = 7.

mastery.prompt: Two x blocks balance 14 pounds. One x equals...

feedback.correct: Exactly, two equal x's share 14, so each is 7.
incorrect: Two matching x blocks share 14 equally.
